title: In Parliament | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP
text_excerpt: In Parliament | Dame Harriett Baldwin MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Harriett About West Worcestershire News Harriett in the press Newsletters In Parliament Treasury Committee FAQs Search Gallery Contact Imagebank In Parliament Dame Harriett’s speeches, debates and questions to Ministers. In Parliament Dame Harriett Baldwin seeks confirmation that no money will be paid to Mauritius over failed treaty 13 April 2026 Now that Parliament has not ratified the treaty on the future of the Diego Garcia Military Base and British Indian Ocean Territory, Dame Harriett Baldwin seeks confirmation that no money will now be paid to the Mauritian Government. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin urges PM to act to protect helium supply for NHS 13 April 2026 Speaking in a debate on the situation in the middle east and the implications for Britain’s security, Dame Harriett Baldwin raises her concerns with the PM that the UK relies on the Gulf for helium which is vital for MRI scans in the NHS. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts PM over 9% council tax hike 25 March 2026 Dame Harriett Baldwin confronts the Prime Minister directly over his Minister’s decision to give permission to Worcestershire County Council to hike council tax by 9%. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin challenges Chancellor over £150bn borrowing as Middle East crisis deepens 24 March 2026 In light of the current crisis in the middle east, Dame Harriett Baldwin asks the Chancellor if she regrets her Budget which increased unfunded borrowing by £150 billion and described by the OBR as one of the largest fiscal loosenings in decades. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin criticises steel strategy as unsustainable 19 March 2026 Shadow Business Minister Dame Harriett Baldwin criticises the Government’s steel strategy as failing to deliver a competitive, sovereign steel industry that can stand on its own two feet, and risks a permanent state-funded drain on taxpayers who are already losing an estimated £1.3 million a day and subsidising £110,000 per job to keep the Scunthorpe blast furnace operational. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin seeks Parliamentary vote on international aid cuts 19 March 2026 Following the Foreign Secretary's statement on the Government’s decision to reduce the international development budget to 0.3% of gross national income (GNI), Dame Harriett Baldwin asks whether aid match programmes will continue. She also highlights that the 0.7% target remains on the statute book and calls on the Government to hold a vote in Parliament on the proposed cuts. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin highlights great West Worcestershire social enterprises 18 March 2026 Shadow Business Minister Dame Harriett Baldwin speaks in a debate on social enterprises and community ownership highlighting the mounting financial pressures resulting from the increases in National Insurance contributions, unresolved business rates pressures, and the additional labour costs imposed by the Employment Rights Act. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin calls for improvements in Royal Mail performance 18 March 2026 Shadow Business Minister Dame Harriett Baldwin winds up a debate on the performance of Royal Mail calling for the service, which is relied upon by millions, to be restored to the standards that the law requires and the public expect. Image Dame Harriett Baldwin presses for heating oil crisis fund details 16 March 2026 Dame Harriett Baldwin makes the case for her constituents struggling with heating oil and LPG costs, presses the Government for specifics on additional funding going into the crisis and resilience fund in West Worcestershire.
